title: Gintama: Jump Festa 2008 Special
titleNative: ジャンプアニメツアー 2008 白夜叉降誕
synopsis: According to Aniplex, a 10min short anime of Gintama "Shiroyasha Koutan" will be released on September 30th. It was shown at Jump Super Anime Tour 2008 and had never been released as a DVD. It's a trailer of an imaginary Gintama movie. Gintama OVA shown at Jump Anime Tour '05 is also included in the DVD.

synopsis: Based on the Russian "national character" Cheburashka. The character comes from Eduard Uspensky's story about a mysterious creature who gets accidentally transported in a box of oranges to Russia.

title: Battle Spirits: Burning Soul
titleNative: バトルスピリッツ 烈火魂
synonyms: バトルスピリッツバーニングソウル
synopsis: In the near future, a battle card game called "Battle Spirits" has gained enormous popularity. Players of the game -- known as "Battlers" -- start duelings everywhere using their color-coded cards with different attributes, creating a "Sengoku (Warring States) period" for the game. (Source: ANN)

title: Kkoma Sinseon Tao
titleNative: 꼬마 신선 타오
synopsis: This Korean and Chinese co-production series focuses on Tao, a young boy with magical powers, as he attends school to become a wizard. Each episode was composed of two stories. (Source: Wiki)

title: A Spirit of the Sun
titleRomaji: Taiyou no Mokushiroku: A Spirit of the Sun
titleNative: 太陽の黙示録 A Spirit of the Sun
synopsis: On August 10 2002, the eruption of Mount Fuji, as well as a series of large-scale earthquakes tsunamis inflict tremendous damage to Japan, sending the country into a state of emergency. Furthermore, another massive earthquake hit Japan on August 15, splitting the country into two. While Japanese refugees were taken in by various countries, the Japanese government accepted aid from China and the United States, which eventually caused China to govern the North, and the USA to govern the South. 15 years later, the Japanese refugees residing in Taiwan and the Taiwanese citizens find themselves clashing with one another, due to job competition and the unimproving conditions of the Japanese refugees. Genichiro Ryu is a survivor of the catastrophic events that took place in Japan, but was raised in Taiwan by a couple who adopted him. He gets himself involved in the situation between the Taiwanese and the Japanese, hoping that the two parties will be able to coexist with one another. (Source:

title: Earthian
titleNative: アーシアン
synopsis: Angels from the planet Eden have been watching Earthians, the inhabitants of Earth, for 5 billion years. Chihaya, an angel with unlucky black hair and wings, is a plus checker who travels to Earth with Kagetsuya, a minus checker. Each team of plus and minus checkers keep track of Earth events and, if the score ever reaches minus 10,000, the Earth will be destroyed. The story follows Chihaya and Kagetsuya and the different people and events they observe on Earth. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: AM Driver
titleNative: Get Ride! アムドライバー
synopsis: In the future, human beings are attacked by beings called the Bug-chine, which appeared a few years back. No normal human weapon can defeat the Bug-chine, but there is hope. It is the AMDrivers that will save the earth. They are solders using the latest "AM Technology". With overwhelming power, they will beat the Bug-chine one after another and become heroes, as well as capture the hearts of their fans. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Combattler V
titleNative: 超電磁ロボ コン・バトラーV
synonyms: Chou Denji Robo Com Battler V
超電磁ロボ コンバトラーＶ
synopsis: The first of Tadao Nagahama's Romance Super Robot Trilogy. (#2: Chou Denji Machine Voltes V, #3: Toushou Daimos) Thousands of years ago, the people of the planet Campbell decided to leave their planet and seek out new worlds to inhabit. One group, lead by the scientist Oreana, landed on Earth, but was delayed from their mission. In the early 21st century, Oreana's group reawakens and begins their plan to conquer the Earth. The only effective defense against the Campbellians' giant biomechanical slave beasts is the super-electromagnetic robot, Combattler V and its pilots. (Source: AniDB)

title: Onipan!
titleNative: おにぱん！
synopsis: Over the centuries, oni (AKA demons of Japanese folklore) got a pretty bad reputation. Now it’s up to three plucky oni kids — sunny Tsutsuji, cool Tsuyukusa and boisterous Himawari — to improve human-oni relations through love, friendship and superpowered underpants! (Source: Sentai Filmworks) Note: Aired in 2 versions, a 60-episode 3-minute short series, and a combined 12-episode 14-minute TV series with the same content. Each episode of the combined version is 5 parts of the original.

title: Touch: CROSS ROAD - Kaze no Yukue
titleRomaji: Touch: CROSS ROAD - Kaze no Yukue
titleNative: タッチ CROSS ROAD～風のゆくえ
synopsis: Trying to find his own game of baseball, and not that of his brothers, Tatsuya moves to America and pitches for a poor, struggling minor league baseball team called the Emeralds. Meanwhile, Minami is still in Japan, trying to find her own path after quitting gymnastics. Inspired by a photograph of her final performance, she becomes the photographers assisstant. (Source: Anime News Network)

title: Danganronpa 3: The End of Hope’s Peak High School - Despair Arc
titleRomaji: Danganronpa 3: The End of Kibougamine Gakuen - Zetsubou-hen
titleEnglish: Danganronpa 3: The End of Hope’s Peak High School - Despair Arc
titleNative: ダンガンロンパ3 -The End of 希望ヶ峰学園-　絶望編
synopsis: Chisa Yukizome begins her job as homeroom teacher for Hope's Peak Academy's 77th Class of Ultmate students. Meanwhile, Hajime Hinata, a student of the school's Reserve Course for students without talent, prepares to undergo an experiment to make him the Ultimate Hope. What follows is a series of tragic events which lead to the birth of the Remnants of Despair and the realization of "The Biggest, Most Awful, Most Tragic Event in Human History." Aired alongside of Danganronpa 3: Mirai-hen, with Mirai-hen starting first.

title: Scoopers
titleNative: スクーパーズ
synopsis: In the 22nd century, the Shambhala city reporter Youko, who works for the Private Eyes newspaper, and her android boyfriend, bodyguard and cameraman Pete are hunting the enigmatic criminal Mr. X. Their search leads them to the Rainbow Tower at the TechnoLand amusement park, where Youko is taken hostage and Pete must battle to save her by destroying the program of the tower's central computer to prevent Mr. X's escape. (Source: AniDB)

title: Mameneko
titleNative: まめねこ
synopsis: The daily life of a household that has two cats: Azuki and Daizu. Azuki and Daizu belong to an office worker in her 30s who loves cats. The owner lives with her father, mother, grandfather, and older brother, the latter of whom the cats call "Megane" (Glasses). Megane adopted a Shiba Inu dog named Mamenosuke after Mamenosuke's owner died. Mamenosuke grew up around cats so he actually thinks of himself as a cat. (Source: Anime News Network)
